title: Compare with multiplication word problems
description: Select the equation that can be used to solve a word problem.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lucy sold $8$ cups of lemonade.  Abbie sold $6$ times as many cups of lemonade.  

**Which equation will help us find how many cups $(a)$ Abbie sold?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
What number is $\greenD6$ times as many as $\maroonD8$?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD6\times\maroonD8=\purpleC{a}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $5$ penguins on Snowy Rock.  There are $7$ times as many penguins on Glacier Point.  

**Which equation will help us find how many penguins $(g)$ are on Glacier Point?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
What number is $\greenD7$ times as many as $\maroonD5$?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD6\times\maroonD8=\purpleC{g}$

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $8$ paintings in the museum.  There are $24$ statues.

There are $\Box$ times as many statues as paintings in the museum. 

**Which equation will help us find the unknown $(\Box)$ number?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
How many $\maroonD{8}$s do we need to make $\purpleC{24}$?

[[☃ image 1]]


$\greenD{\Box}\times \maroonD8 = \purpleC{24}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Tanya ate $5$ bananas this week.  Her pet monkey ate $35$ bananas this week.  

Tanya's pet monkey ate $\Box$ times as many bananas as Tanya this week.

**Which equation will help us find the unknown $(\Box)$ 
 number?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
How many $\maroonD{5}$s do you need to make $\purpleC{35}$?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{\Box}\times \maroonD5 = \purpleC{35}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Luke and Katie were picking strawberries.  Katie picked  $36$ strawberries.  She picked $6$ times as many strawberries as Luke picked.

**Which equation will help us find how many strawberries $(L)$ Luke picked?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
If we divide $\purpleC{36}$ into $\greenD6$ equal groups, how big is each group?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{6}\times \maroonD{L} = \purpleC{36}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Enzo saw $45$ squirrels at the park.  This was $9$ times as many squirrels as he saw in his yard.

**Which equation will help us find how many squirrels $(y)$ Enzo saw in his yard?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
If we divide $\purpleC{45}$ into $\greenD9$ equal groups, how big is each group?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{9}\times \maroonD{y} = \purpleC{45}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Rhonda the rabbit found $2$ carrots.  Her friend Bob found $5$ times as many carrots.

**How many carrots did Bob find?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Rhonda found $\maroonD{2}$ carrots.

Let's say Bob found $\purpleC{b}$ carrots.

$\greenD{5} \times \maroonD{2}=\purpleC{b}$

[[☃ image 1]] 

What number is $\greenD5$ times as many as $\maroonD2$?

$\greenD{5} \times \maroonD{2}=\purpleC{10}$

[[☃ image 1]]
Bob found $10$ carrots.

assessment_items.question_content:
Every morning $10$ elephants come to the watering hole.  In the evening $4$ times as many elephants come.

**How many elephants come to the watering hole in the evening?**   

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In the morning, $\maroonD{10}$ elephants come.

Let's say that in the evening, $\purpleC{e}$ elephants come.

$\greenD{4} \times \maroonD{10}=\purpleC{e}$

[[☃ image 1]]

What number is $\greenD4$ times as many as $\maroonD{10}$?

$\greenD{4} \times \maroonD{10}=\purpleC{40}$

[[☃ image 1]]
$40$ elephants come to the watering hole in the evening.

assessment_items.question_content:
Miguel has lots of candy from Halloween.  He has $42$ lollipops. He has $6$ times as many lollipops as he does chocolates.

**How many chocolates does Miguel have?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Miguel has $\purpleC{42}$ lollipops.

Let's say Miguel has $\maroonD{c}$ chocolates.

$\greenD6~\times~\maroonD{c} = \purpleC{42}$

[[☃ image 1]]

If we divide $\purpleC{42}$ into $\greenD6$ equal groups, how big is each group?

$\greenD6\times\maroonD{7} = \purpleC{42}$

[[☃ image 1]]
Miguel has $7$ chocolates.

assessment_items.question_content:
Naveen has a bag of marbles.  There are $44$ green marbles.  He has $4$ times as many green marbles as he does blue marbles.

**How many blue marbles does Naveen have?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Naveen has $\purpleC{44}$ green marbles.

Let's say Naveen has $\maroonD{b}$ blue marbles.

$\greenD4~\times~\maroonD{b} = \purpleC{44}$

[[☃ image 1]]

If we divide $\purpleC{44}$ into $\greenD4$ equal groups, how big is each group?

$\greenD4\times\maroonD{11} = \purpleC{44}$

[[☃ image 1]]
Naveen has $\maroonD{11}$ blue marbles.

assessment_items.question_content:
Shun caught $35$ fish.  Hiroto caught $7$ fish.

**Complete the comparison.**

Shun caught [[☃ dropdown 1]] times as many fish as Hiroto caught.
hints:
Shun caught $\purpleC{35}$ fish.

Hiroto caught $\maroonD{7}$ fish.

$\greenD{?}\times\maroonD{7} = \purpleC{35}$

[[☃ image 1]]

How many groups of $\maroonD{7}$ would it take to equal $\purpleC{35}$ ?

$\greenD5\times\maroonD{7} = \purpleC{35}$

[[☃ image 1]]
Shun caught $5$ times as many fish as Hiroto caught.

assessment_items.question_content:
A hummingbird visited $35$ flowers. A bee visited $70$ flowers.

**Complete the comparison.**  

The bee visited [[☃ dropdown 1]] times as many flowers as the hummingbird visited.
hints:
The bee visited $\purpleC{70}$ flowers.

The hummingbird visited $\maroonD{35}$ flowers.

$\greenD{{?}} \times \maroonD{35} = \purpleC{70}$

[[☃ image 1]]

How many groups of $\maroonD{35}$ would it take to equal $\purpleC{70}$ ?

$\greenD2\times\maroonD{35} = \purpleC{70}$

[[☃ image 1]]
The bee visited $2$ times as many flowers as the hummingbird visited.
